root_cause: Checkout API pods exhausted their local PostgreSQL connection pools after version 2026.05.14.3 rolled out with a reduced max pool size. The logs alone suggested a generic database slowdown, but the decisive signal was that database host connection utilization stayed normal while application-side pool waiters and checkout latency spiked immediately after the deployment.

details: Repeated connection acquisition warnings and context deadline exceeded errors began at 16:05Z
No matching PostgreSQL node health or failover errors appeared in the same window
checkout_api.db_pool_waiters increased from 0 to 58 while postgres_primary.active_connections_percent stayed near 50 percent
checkout_api.p95_latency_ms and error rate spiked within one five-minute bucket of the deploy
Commit 91fb2c8 normalized DB pool settings across environments and packaged the reduced pool limit
Production rollout completed at 15:59:22Z and rollback at 16:29:54Z restored the previous version

recommended_actions: Restore the previous max connection pool size and validate environment-specific overrides before rollout
Alert on application pool waiter growth separately from database host saturation
Add deployment-time config diff checks for connection pool settings
expected_investigation_path: search logs for latency and timeout symptoms
compare application-side pool saturation metrics with database host utilization
inspect recent commits touching database configuration
correlate impact onset with the 15:59Z deployment and confirm rollback recovery
